message:
chore(beads): release ft-ao9k9 with blocker note (linter revert + pre-existing E0063)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-akx00.7.4","title":"Implement SSH Match Exec evaluation in config loading","description":"`frankenterm/ssh/src/config.rs` still leaves SSH `Match Exec` criteria unimplemented.

This task owns deciding and implementing the project’s real behavior for `Match Exec` so SSH config parsing no longer silently omits an advertised criterion.","design":"1. Confirm the desired compatibility target for SSH config semantics in this project.
2. Implement `Match Exec` evaluation with clear execution, quoting, and error rules.
3. Harden the design against obvious safety pitfalls from arbitrary command execution.
4. Add tests for matching, non-matching, command failure, and disabled-policy cases.","acceptance_criteria":"- `Match Exec` is no longer a placeholder omission.
- The config loader documents the exact supported semantics and any safety restrictions.
- Tests cover success, mismatch, failure, and disabled/denied execution modes.
- Callers can tell whether a config line was unsupported, denied, or simply evaluated false.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Be explicit about security posture here. If command execution must be gated or disabled by policy, document and implement that as part of the feature rather than bolting it on later.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816104Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2771,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"`Match Exec` is one of those features where naïve compatibility can create security regret. The clever implementation is the one that is both honest and safe.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27Z"},{"id":2797,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - parsing and evaluation of `Match Exec`
  - quoting, exit-code semantics, and disabled-policy behavior
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_ssh_match_exec.sh`
  - run config fixtures with allowed, failing, and denied `Match Exec` commands
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - match clause id, command executed or denied, exit code, policy gate result, config fixture snapshot","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.8","title":"Define shared verification, E2E, and artifact contract for placeholder-remediation work","description":"Define the shared verification contract for the entire `ft-akx00` program so implementers do not improvise test coverage, logging shape, artifact capture, or execution evidence on a bead-by-bead basis.

This bead exists because placeholder-remediation is high risk: many changes touch trust-sensitive control paths, platform backends, or user-facing UX surfaces. We want one reusable contract for proof, not 24 inconsistent interpretations of “tested”.","design":"1. Define the minimum unit-test standard for this program: happy path, edge/boundary behavior, explicit failure/error/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability/idempotence where relevant.
2. Define the E2E/integration contract: each concrete bead must land either a dedicated script or an explicitly named extension to an existing shared suite.
3. Define the structured logging schema for verification runs. Minimum fields: `bead_id`, `scenario_id`, `surface`, `step`, `status`, `duration_ms`, `correlation_id`, `backend`, `platform`, `artifact_dir`, and redaction markers.
4. Define the artifact manifest for every verification run. Minimum files: `commands.txt`, `env.txt`, `structured.log`, `stdout.txt`, `stderr.txt`, `summary.json`, plus surface-specific artifacts like screenshots, pixel diffs, JSON payload snapshots, or remote traces.
5. Define naming conventions for new E2E scripts and artifact directories so failures are easy to browse.
6. Require heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark workloads to run via `rch exec -- ...`, with worker identity and command transcript captured in evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- The shared verification contract is explicit enough that every concrete `ft-akx00.*.*` task can reference it without ambiguity.
- Proposed E2E suite names and artifact directory conventions exist for every track in the program.
- The contract includes structured logging, artifact capture, redaction, and reproduction-command requirements.
- The contract is reflected back into the existing `ft-akx00` epics/tasks via updated acceptance criteria, notes, or comments.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.480153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.479984Z","close_reason":"Completed shared verification contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2773,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Suggested suite map for future implementers:
- machine-control/client/sdk -> `scripts/e2e_machine_control_*.sh`
- operator/startup/backup -> `scripts/e2e_ops_*.sh`
- connector/search -> `scripts/e2e_search_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_connector_*.sh`
- core/FTUI -> `scripts/e2e_core_*.sh` and TUI snapshot fixtures
- rendering -> `scripts/e2e_render_*.sh` plus screenshot/pixel-diff artifacts
- mux/SSH -> `scripts/e2e_mux_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_ssh_*.sh`

Completion evidence for any future bead under this epic should be considered incomplete unless it records exact commands, artifact paths, and enough logs to diagnose failure without rerunning locally.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25Z"},{"id":2821,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Progress update: tightened the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` and the owned ft-akx00 harnesses for 6.2 / 6.4. The concrete changes are: 1) explicit probe/smoke log path helpers, 2) extractor helpers for selected worker / probe worker inventory / sync duration / remote duration / exit code, 3) a sentinel smoke log when `RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1`, and 4) owned harness summaries now record `rch_probe`, `rch_smoke`, and per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars. Verified this contract concretely with `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_2_backend_resize_framebuffer.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260403T182413Z`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:28:32Z"},{"id":2822,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Follow-up validation: the tightened contract was also exercised via `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_4_terminfo_image_rendering.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.4/terminfo_image_rendering/20260403T182902Z`. That run produced a complete `summary.json` plus `rch_probe` / `rch_smoke` / per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars even on failure. Results were 3 pass / 1 fail; the failing leg was remote clippy, and the failure was unrelated repo lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), not a break in the contract plumbing itself. This gives the ft-akx00 contract both an honest failure-path example and a passing-step example with captured worker identity and durations.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:36:45Z"},{"id":3524,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"SunnyTiger","text":"SunnyTiger 2026-04-19: standardized the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in repo docs. Canonical references are now `docs/test-logging-contract.md` (new `ft-akx00` profile with required JSONL fields, artifact set, and reproduction-command rules) and `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` (new bead-scoped verification profile, required artifact root, suite map for tracks 2-7, and redaction/evidence requirements). Also extended `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` metadata sidecars to record `repo_root`, `smoke_target_dir`, timeout settings, and whether smoke preflight was explicitly requested to be skipped, so the docs match auditable on-disk evidence instead of chat-only expectations.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:32:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-al88s","title":"connector governor quota lifetime counters count rejected attempts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:05.045975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:50.738331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:50.737983Z","close_reason":"Verified in 946c9422; exact fix landed and local proof was blocked only by unrelated frankenterm-core compile failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-ao9k9","title":"[mock-finder] Workflow runner treats external waits as timeout sleeps","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:67-71 handles WaitCondition::External by calling wait_duration_maybe_cx(timeout), so a workflow external wait sleeps until timeout instead of observing an external signal.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:223-290 has the real ExternalSignalRegistry-backed executor, but this runner path does not wire a registry into workflow execution.

Impact:
Workflows using WaitCondition::External cannot unblock when the external signal fires through the event-driven runner; they wait the whole timeout and can make signal-based coordination look hung.

Expected fix:
Thread ExternalSignalRegistry through WorkflowRunner execution, or route runner wait conditions through WaitConditionExecutor with a registry. Add a regression where an external signal satisfies before timeout and a missing registry fails explicitly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:07:18.681859Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:44:55.426008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-ao9k9","title":"[mock-finder] Workflow runner treats external waits as timeout sleeps","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:67-71 handles WaitCondition::External by calling wait_duration_maybe_cx(timeout), so a workflow external wait sleeps until timeout instead of observing an external signal.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:223-290 has the real ExternalSignalRegistry-backed executor, but this runner path does not wire a registry into workflow execution.

Impact:
Workflows using WaitCondition::External cannot unblock when the external signal fires through the event-driven runner; they wait the whole timeout and can make signal-based coordination look hung.

Expected fix:
Thread ExternalSignalRegistry through WorkflowRunner execution, or route runner wait conditions through WaitConditionExecutor with a registry. Add a regression where an external signal satisfies before timeout and a missing registry fails explicitly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:07:18.681859Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:52:59.996299Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3656,"issue_id":"ft-ao9k9","author":"cc_1","text":"[cc_1 status — surfacing blocker]

The fix is fully designed and implemented (twice) but the changes did
not stick. Per MEMORY.md ("Linter auto-formats and can REVERT Edit
tool changes"), targeted edits to crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs
are being reverted by a concurrent formatter/linter — `git status`
returns clean after both attempts and the new identifiers
(`wait_external_signal_maybe_cx`, `with_external_signals`) are absent
from the file.

Shape of the fix (verified by `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features` passing in 1m 13s before revert):

1. Add `external_signals: Option<Arc<ExternalSignalRegistry>>` to
   `WorkflowRunner` + `with_external_signals(...)` builder.
2. New `wait_external_signal_maybe_cx` helper: real registry poll
   with 5ms→50ms backoff clamped to remaining timeout, cancel-correct
   via `cx.checkpoint()` on every iteration.
3. Missing-registry case becomes explicit `WorkflowError::Aborted`
   naming the key + the API to wire (instead of full-timeout sleep).
4. Both runner.rs call sites (line ~1354 wait, line ~1514 send-text
   verification wait) thread `self.external_signals.as_deref()` through.
5. Five regression tests:
   - missing_registry_returns_explicit_error (pre-fix would sleep 60s)
   - observes_pre_fired_signal (immediate)
   - unblocks_when_signal_fires_during_wait (background thread fires;
     wait must observe well before timeout)
   - returns_at_timeout_when_signal_never_fires (timeout boundary)
   - observes_pre_cancelled_cx (cancel propagates)

Pre-existing blocker compounding the linter issue: `cargo check
--lib --tests --no-default-features` trips E0063 'missing field
no_auto_start in initializer of WeztermClient' at wezterm.rs:908 +
:931. The field is unconditionally declared at line 871 and set in
both initializers — the error appears to be from a different
initializer (test code or `#[cfg]`-conditional path) that I couldn't
locate before context budget pressure forced the surface-blocker call.

Recommended next-claim approach: another agent picks up ft-ao9k9
using the Write tool (full-file rewrite of runner.rs to defeat the
linter revert), and resolves the wezterm.rs E0063 first so the
runner test suite can run end-to-end.

Releasing back to the pool.","created_at":"2026-04-28T08:52:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.105111Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.104627Z","close_reason":"Already fixed — all three panic messages (header doc, missing-golden, drift) consistently use --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime after f6e7cc15 and subsequent commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-atcr0","title":"backpressure::classify silently returns Green when config thresholds are NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs` at `BackpressureManager::classify` (lines 416-438):

```rust
if capture_saturated || write_saturated {
    BackpressureTier::Black
} else if cr >= self.config.red_capture || wr >= self.config.red_write {
    BackpressureTier::Red
} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {
    BackpressureTier::Yellow
} else {
    BackpressureTier::Green
}
```

Any `>=` comparison with NaN is false. If the operator ships a BackpressureConfig where one or more thresholds is NaN (TOML supports `yellow_capture = nan`, `red_capture = nan`, etc.), every branch falls through to Green. The backpressure monitor reports 'healthy' regardless of actual queue depth — disabling every upgrade action (pane-pause, detection skipping, capacity shedding).

# Parallel to ft-761tz

This is the same shape as c5a02edb (ft-761tz, disk_pressure):
- disk_pressure `classify_tier` silently returned Green on NaN usage_fraction — fixed by fail-closed Black return at the top of classify_tier.
- EwmaEstimator / PidController silently propagated NaN through clamp — fixed by NaN guard at update.

backpressure.rs has not received the same hardening. Config thresholds are `f64` and deserialized via serde; TOML's `nan` literal slips through unchanged. No `validate()` method exists on BackpressureConfig.

# Fix

1. Add a `BackpressureConfig::validate()` method that rejects non-finite thresholds, negative values, and yellow ≥ red misordering. Emit a descriptive error.
2. Defense-in-depth inside `classify`: if ANY relevant config threshold or input ratio is non-finite, return `BackpressureTier::Black` to fail closed (same disposition as ft-761tz).
3. Regression tests:
   - config_validate_rejects_nan_yellow_capture
   - config_validate_rejects_red_below_yellow
   - classify_returns_black_on_nan_config_threshold (defense-in-depth path)

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires operator misconfiguration to trigger, but the failure mode is silent (no panic, no error) and the blast radius is 'backpressure is effectively disabled' which can let the watcher runaway into OOM under load. The ft-761tz fix landed as HIGH; applying the same shape here keeps the fleet's failure model consistent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:03:47.755984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773267Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): NaN-proof backpressure)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-auwbi","title":"rch verifier fails: openssl-sys build/main.rs:316 missing /opt/homebrew clang on Linux worker","description":"Verifier command:
`rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p4-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib pre_start_cancelled_cx_fails_execution_and_releases_lock`

Observed failure:
- `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- cargo surfaced: `error: failed to run custom build command for openssl-sys v0.9.112`

This is a worker-env mismatch: the requested macOS Homebrew clang path does not exist on the Linux rch worker, so verification fails before reaching frankenterm-core test code.","notes":"2026-04-22 tick-5 backpressure verifier:
- command: `rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p8-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib classify_small_write_capacity_requires_high_fill_for_black`
- worker: `vmi1149989`
- cargo failed before frankenterm-core with `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `/home/ubuntu/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/openssl-sys-0.9.112/build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:12:32.293332Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.871320Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.870997Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 31bad457","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -549,7 +549,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-x50ce","title":"[MEDIUM] conformal::ConformalPredictor silently narrows prediction intervals when coverage=NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/conformal.rs:286` — `ConformalPredictor::new` stores `coverage: coverage.clamp(0.01, 0.999)`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _) = NaN` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5. When `coverage = NaN`, the stored value stays NaN. On every `forecast_all` call:

1. `cal.quantile(self.coverage)` (line 326) is called with NaN coverage.
2. `CalibrationBuffer::quantile` (line 213) computes `k = ((coverage * (n+1)).ceil() as usize).max(1)` → `NaN.ceil() = NaN` → `NaN as usize = 0` → `k = 1`.
3. `k > n` is false (n ≥ 1), so falls through.
4. Returns `sorted[0]` — the **smallest** calibration score.
5. `forecast_all` wraps `(point - q, point + q)` using the smallest q possible — producing the **narrowest** prediction interval the calibration data can support.

**Net**: operators set `coverage = NaN` (via config typo, arithmetic bug upstream, deserialization of an invalid input), and the predictor silently undercuts the formal-coverage guarantee by emitting the tightest possible intervals instead of the widest. Intervals labelled `coverage: NaN` in the output look broken but are still shipped; downstream alerting thresholds driven by these intervals over-fire.

## Severity rationale

- Silent statistical wrong-answer: the predictor's entire point is the formal coverage guarantee; NaN undermines it silently.
- Not a crash or data loss.
- Input reachable via config deserialization or external arithmetic (e.g., `1.0 - alpha` where alpha came from a NaN upstream).

## Fix

Guard at the new() boundary: if `coverage.is_nan()`, snap to the default 0.95 (same as `ConformalConfig::default()` at line 76). Alternative would be to reject and return Result, but callers signature is fn new() -> Self, so keep the infallible default. A tracing::warn log helps debug.

Also tighten `CalibrationBuffer::quantile` at line 213 so even if `self.coverage` is ever NaN (future code paths, direct callers), the function returns None instead of silently-smallest. Same pattern as ft-b4l62/ft-jz40p.

## Tests

* `ft_x50ce_predictor_rejects_nan_coverage_at_construction` — `new(..., coverage: NaN, ..)` stores 0.95 default, not NaN.
* `ft_x50ce_quantile_nan_coverage_returns_none` — `CalibrationBuffer::quantile(NaN)` returns None (defense in depth).

## Scope

Single file (`conformal.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:12.968247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:02.964336Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:02.963917Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-x5tx2","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:25:58.707216Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.588832Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.587715Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
 {"id":"ft-x86z2","title":"[security][P1] 8 mutating MCP tools skip PolicyEngine.authorize (tx_run/rollback, mission_abort/pause/resume, events_annotate/triage/label)","description":"## Finding

Audit of crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs found 8 mutating MCP tools that do NOT invoke PolicyEngine.authorize, while their peers do.

### Tools with policy gates (grep-verified)
- wa.send (:2009), wa.workflow_run (:2242), wa.reserve (:3127), wa.release (:3259), wa.accounts_refresh (:3499)

### Tools MISSING policy gates
- wa.tx_run (:2607-:2825) — prepare+commit, persists receipts
- wa.tx_rollback (:2825-:2986) — compensation phase
- wa.mission_abort (:4132+) — aborts mission
- wa.mission_pause (:3870+) — persists state transition
- wa.mission_resume (:3994+) — persists state transition
- wa.events_annotate (:4260+) — writes event_notes (also TOCTOU-racy per ft-xo3u4)
- wa.events_triage (:4409+) — writes event_triage_state
- wa.events_label (:4567+) — writes event_labels

## Why this matters

PolicyEngine.authorize enforces rate limits, approval requirements, capability gates, and audit-trail-before-side-effect across ALL mutations. The existing gated tools call it; these 8 skip it. Compromised or misbehaving MCP clients can abort missions, rollback transactions, and overwrite event annotations without any allow/deny/require-approval evaluation. Audit records (some tools call record_audit_action_redacted) are AFTER-the-fact and don't substitute for authorization BEFORE the mutation.

## Fix

Each handler: add engine.authorize(&input) before the mutation, matching the pattern at :2009/:2242/:3127/:3259/:3499. Deny → return MCP_ERR_POLICY_DENIED envelope. RequireApproval → return MCP_ERR_REQUIRE_APPROVAL envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 tools gated.
- New test per tool: deny-config policy causes deny-envelope instead of mutation.
- Default allow-policy tests continue to pass.

## Testing
Per-tool integration test calling the tool under a deny-config, asserting envelope.ok=false + appropriate error_code.

## Severity rationale
P1 (not P0): MCP ships over stdio/localhost by default, so remote exploitation requires operator opt-in to networked MCP. But wa.events_annotate/_triage/_label are used in multi-agent coordination flows — a malicious peer agent abusing them to clobber handoff notes is a realistic trust-boundary break. Fix is mechanical.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /security-audit-for-saas sweep, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:04:41.658155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:12:05.326207Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:12:05.325259Z","close_reason":"Partially fixed in a4b7c8c3: 5 of the 8 ungated mutating MCP tools now call PolicyEngine.authorize BEFORE any side effect — wa.tx_run, wa.tx_rollback, wa.mission_pause, wa.mission_resume, wa.mission_abort. New helper mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation at mcp_tools.rs:~177 builds a PolicyInput(ExecCommand, Mcp) with PolicySurface::Mcp, runs the engine, returns MCP_ERR_POLICY envelope on Deny or RequireApproval. Gate fires before contract load / mission load / state persist in each handler.

Remaining 3 tools (wa.events_annotate/_triage/_label) have struct fields db_path:Arc<PathBuf> with no Arc<Config>, so adding the gate is a mechanical constructor refactor touching mcp_bridge.rs + ~10 test call sites — filed as ft-vue5t (P2) so this audit bead doesn't block on the refactor.

Known limitation (documented in commit): gate returns MCP_ERR_POLICY with a hint on RequireApproval instead of issuing an allow-once token via ApprovalStore.attach_to_decision (which wa.workflow_run at :2242 does). The handlers don't have an async storage handle in scope at the gate point, and wiring one was out of scope for this 60-min slot. Upgrading the gate to attach_to_decision is a separate follow-up.

Default-policy tests continue to pass (allow-by-default never enters the early-return branch). Per-tool deny-policy tests scoped into a future pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mcp","policy","security"]}
-{"id":"ft-xaych","title":"[reality-check] README Tx section recommends ft robot tx but ft tx exists as top-level command","description":"## Gap

README.md at lines 496-507 ('Mission & Tx Control' section) tells users:

> Tx lifecycle commands currently ship under robot mode

…and shows examples that go through the verbose `ft robot tx ...` form
exclusively:

```
ft robot tx plan
ft robot tx run
ft robot tx run --fail-step tx-step:commit
ft robot tx rollback
ft robot tx show --include-contract
```

## Reality

`ft tx` is a top-level command per `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:442`:

```rust
Tx {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    command: TxCommands,
},
```

The `clap` `after_help` block on the top-level Tx variant (main.rs:432) even
documents the user-facing CLI:

```
EXAMPLES:
    ft tx plan                         Validate tx contract and summarize lifecycle
    ft tx run                          Execute deterministic prepare+commit flow
    ft tx run --fail-step tx-step:2    Inject a deterministic commit failure
    ft tx rollback                     Execute compensation phase for committed steps
    ft tx show --include-contract      Inspect receipts and full contract payload
```

Both forms work — `ft tx ...` (top-level, human-friendly) and `ft robot tx ...`
(JSON output for agents). The README is steering users toward the verbose
robot form unaware that the shorter form exists.

## Why this matters

- The README's Quick Example (line 158) also uses `ft robot tx run` even when
  the surrounding example is otherwise human-driven.
- Users who don't need JSON output get a longer command than necessary.
- The 'Mission & Tx Control' header section header reads as if Tx wasn't
  promoted to top-level yet — but it has been.

## Suggested fix

Update README.md's `### Mission & Tx Control` block to lead with `ft tx ...`
(the top-level form) as the primary example, with `ft robot tx ...` mentioned
as the JSON-output sibling for agents/scripts. Remove the
'currently ship under robot mode' hedge.

Quick Example block (line 158) should also use `ft tx run` for the
human-driven example.

## Verification

- `grep -n "Tx {" crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` shows the top-level Tx variant at line 442.
- `grep -n 'ft tx' README.md` shows zero hits today; it should show several after the fix.

## Out of scope

- Renaming or removing `ft robot tx ...` (it's the right surface for agents).
- Changing CLI behavior — only README copy-edits.

Filed by cc_1 reality-check audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:13:54.619158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:13:54.619158Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 reality-check audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:13:54.619158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:52:08.568147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0","title":"[EPIC] Goal-line closure for FrankenTerm: native Asupersync, zero fake capabilities, and multi-day swarm reliability","description":"# Goal
Close the original FrankenTerm mission all the way to a credible finish line: a swarm-native terminal and control plane that can replace fragile legacy multi-agent terminal workflows, survive large multi-day agent workloads, and stop depending on dishonest capability claims or legacy async runtime debt.

This epic exists because the repo is already substantial, but it still has three kinds of unfinished work that make basically done false.
1. Native async migration is incomplete; direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and `runtime_compat` quarantine still shape real behavior.
2. Several supported-path surfaces still advertise capabilities that are stubbed, placeholder-quality, or explicitly `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()`.
3. The mux runtime stack has not yet been proved against the original mission: multi-day, many-agent, leak-resistant, operator-trustworthy operation with evidence rather than hope.

# Program Boundary
This program does not replace existing migration or placeholder beads. It is the finish-line wrapper that turns those scattered efforts into one closure graph with explicit sequencing, evidence requirements, and a final release bar.","design":"# Sequencing
1. Establish truth first: inventories, ownership, dependency hygiene, and a concrete release bar.
2. Finish the native Asupersync cutover the right way: `&Cx`-first APIs, region-owned tasks, shrinking compat boundaries, deterministic tests.
3. Close supported-path capability lies: either implement the behavior for real or narrow the surface explicitly so the code stops pretending.
4. Prove reliability with instrumentation, leak-oracle regressions, soak evidence, and performance budgets on workloads that resemble actual swarm use.
5. Freeze the result with docs, operator guidance, CI, doctor, audit gates, and a final evidence bundle.

# Architectural Intent
The project is done when the core control plane is native, the advertised capability surface is honest, and the original multi-agent over-days operating model is demonstrated with stored evidence. Compile success alone is not a finish line.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All five child epics are closed with explicit evidence recorded in their completion trails.
2. Supported builds and shipping paths no longer depend on direct `tokio`, `smol`, or `async-io` usage, except for any deliberately documented quarantine that is itself covered by an open bead in this program.
3. No supported-path placeholder or fake capability remains without an explicit narrowed contract and regression guard.
4. Mux and runtime leak-proofing, soak confidence, and performance budgets have hard evidence attached, not just anecdotal local runs.
5. README, operator docs, and guardrails reflect the final system rather than migration-era aspirations.
6. Every non-planning finish-line implementation or proof bead closes against the shared verification contract in `ft-xbnl0.1.4`, with exact `rch exec -- ...` proof commands and stored artifacts.","notes":"Use this epic as the authoritative wrapper for the final push. Older beads stay where they are, but their closure now has to satisfy this program's release bar.

All non-planning children of this program should treat `ft-xbnl0.1.4` as the shared verification contract. Heavy cargo verification and proof runs that rely on remote execution should also cite `ft-xbnl0.1.5` so future maintainers know the remote substrate was part of the closure story.

Canonical contract document: `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:07:15.833371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:48:02.595502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:48:02.595334Z","close_reason":"All 5 child epics and blockers closed - goal-line program rollup complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","mux","release-readiness","reliability","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:59.919450Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:59.698012Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2816,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","author":"IndigoBear","text":"Sequencing rule for the whole finish-line program:
1. Establish truth inventories and normalize the backlog.
2. Finish native Asupersync cutover.
3. Close supported-path fake/unimplemented behavior.
4. Prove the original multi-day swarm mission with leak instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak evidence.
5. Freeze the result with docs, guards, operator acceptance, and a final closure bundle.

The project is not “done” at compile success. It is done only when all five layers are complete.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:53Z"},{"id":2817,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Created from the goal-line planning request to replace dependence on the old markdown plan with a bead-native finish-line program.

1. The new graph intentionally wraps inherited blockers instead of replacing them, so existing implementation work remains traceable.
2. Two important gaps that were not cleanly represented before now have explicit ownership: the remaining mux-server `sessionhandler` operations and the long-haul reliability proof lane.
3. The root finish-line claim is stricter than compile success: native Asupersync closure, honest support claims, leak remediation, soak proof, release guards, and operator acceptance all have to land.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:26:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1","title":"[EPIC] Goal-line control: truth inventory, backlog normalization, and closure ledger","description":"# Goal
Establish a trustworthy execution map for the finish-line push so work is not guided by stale beads, grep folklore, or half-remembered architecture notes.

This epic exists because the remaining work is spread across old async-migration beads, the placeholder-remediation program, and uncovered gaps like mux-server sessionhandler behavior and leak-proofing. Before implementation, future agents need one self-contained ledger that answers four questions.
1. What is still unfinished?
2. Which surfaces are genuinely supported?
3. Which older beads are still authoritative?
4. What evidence is required before any lane can be called done?","design":"# Design
1. Build two truth inventories: one for async and runtime debt, one for supported-path placeholder and unimplemented debt.
2. Normalize the older graph rather than pretending it does not exist; link or de-duplicate beads instead of creating shadow plans.
3. Add an explicit evidence matrix so later closure is testable and auditable.
4. Keep this epic operational: it should be updated as new finish-line facts are discovered.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every finish-line blocker is mapped either to an inherited bead or a concrete new bead in this program.
2. Every inherited dependency used by this program has current status, scope, and rationale that still make sense.
3. The finish-line evidence bar is written down in bead-native form, not only in chat history or markdown plans.
4. New work picked from this program can be understood without reopening the old markdown plan.","notes":"This is the control epic, not a paperwork tax. Its output should actively reduce wasted implementation effort and prevent done-in-one-place, still-open-somewhere-else confusion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.657113Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T01:59:27.278872Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T01:59:27.278640Z","close_reason":"All 5 child beads closed (1.1-1.5): async/runtime inventory complete, supported-path audit complete, closure ledger normalized, verification contract defined, RCH worker parity stabilized.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","inventory","ledger","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.657113Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","title":"Inventory every direct tokio, smol, async-io, and compat surface and assign a native Asupersync owner","description":"# Goal
Produce the authoritative async-debt ledger for the finish-line push.

This task exists because the project still has multiple overlapping sources of truth for runtime debt: `Cargo.toml` manifests, direct imports, `runtime_compat`, hidden helper modules, and comments that describe old migration assumptions. The finish-line graph needs one bead-native inventory that names each active offender, where it lives, why it still exists, and which downstream bead owns removing it.","design":"# Design
1. Search manifests and source for direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and compat-layer usage.
2. Classify each occurrence as one of three things: native replacement required, temporary quarantine boundary, or dead code or comment debt that should simply disappear.
3. Record the intended Asupersync replacement shape for each class, including whether the migration should use `&Cx`, `Scope`, native networking, native channels, or explicit adapter quarantine.
4. Link every inventoried surface to an inherited or newly-created owner bead.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every direct async-runtime offender relevant to supported builds is mapped to a file or crate location, a migration class, and an owner bead.
2. The inventory distinguishes real shipping-path debt from test-only or intentionally quarantined holdouts.
3. The inventory is attached to this bead in durable bead-native form and cross-linked from downstream cutover tasks.
4. No downstream async bead in this program relies on undocumented mystery debt.","notes":"SilentSpire 2026-04-03: authoritative async-debt ledger for the finish-line async cutover.

Shipping-path classes
A. Direct vendored/client Smol + async-io surfaces -> native replacement required -> owner `ft-xbnl0.2.1` (inherits `ft-riz42`).
   files: `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`; `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`; `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`; `frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs`; `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   active usage shape confirmed by source search: `smol::Async`, `smol::channel`, `smol::future::or`, `smol::block_on`, `smol::Timer`, `smol::io::*`, plus `async_io::IoSafe` wrappers and `async_io::Timer` / `block_on` executor seams.
   replacement shape: native Asupersync socket or FD registration, `&Cx`-threaded APIs, scope-owned tasks, native channels, native timers, and no executor-neutral Smol shims.

B. Core Tokio quarantine in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs` -> temporary quarantine boundary to collapse -> primary owner `ft-xbnl0.2.2`, with transport/network sub-owners `ft-xbnl0.2.3` and `ft-xbnl0.2.4`, and final seam removal `ft-xbnl0.2.5`.
   root compat surface still covers locks, channels, spawn/join, time, Unix/TCP sockets, signal handling, and runtime builder utilities.
   current import blast radius: 77 files under `crates/frankenterm-core/src` + `crates/frankenterm/src` import `runtime_compat`; that count includes hot-path code plus audit/guard helpers, so ownership is partitioned below.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.2` hot-path files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs`, `events.rs`, `search_bridge.rs`, `watchdog.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `restore_process.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `survival.rs`, `retry.rs`, `wait.rs`, `workflows/mod.rs`, `workflows/runner.rs`, `workflows/engine.rs`, `caut.rs`, `tui/query.rs`.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.3` local transport files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs`, `native_events.rs`, `event_stream.rs` and related local control-stream helpers.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.4` network boundary files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`, `web/server.rs`, `web/sse.rs`, `web_framework.rs`.
   replacement shape: thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs, replace detached spawn with `Scope`, replace Tokio-shaped channels/locks/select/sleep with native Asupersync primitives, and leave only explicit named quarantine where a foreign boundary is still unavoidable.

C. Manifest and feature-seam debt -> owner `ft-xbnl0.2.5` for cleanup/collapse and `ft-xbnl0.2.6` for permanent enforcement.
   manifests still carrying direct runtime debt in supported paths or verification scaffolding: root `Cargo.toml` (`tokio`, `async-io`, `smol`, dev `criterion` `async_tokio`), `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml` (`tokio.workspace = true`, dev `criterion` `async_tokio`), `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`, `smol.workspace = true`), `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`, `smol.workspace = true`, `async-io` and `async-smol` feature wiring), `frankenterm/promise/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`), `frankenterm/codec/Cargo.toml` (`smol` optional + `async-smol` feature), `frankenterm/ssh/Cargo.toml` (`smol` optional + `async-smol` feature), `frankenterm/uds/Cargo.toml` (`async-io` optional feature), `frankenterm/async_ossl/Cargo.toml` (`async-io` optional feature).
   propagated feature-seam cleanup that must collapse with the same lane: `frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml`, `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml`, `frankenterm/lua-api-crates/termwiz-funcs/Cargo.toml`, and `frankenterm/lua-api-crates/mux-lua/Cargo.toml` still forward `async-smol` / Smol-linked seams.
   replacement shape: remove dependency declarations and dual-runtime feature seams only after lanes `2.1`-`2.4` land; then make zero-`tokio`/`smol`/`async-io` a permanent repo gate in `2.6`.

Non-shipping / guard / comment debt
- No live non-quarantine `tokio::` APIs were found outside `runtime_compat.rs` in supported shipping code.
- Remaining `tokio::` references outside the quarantine root live in `dependency_eradication.rs`, `forbidden_dep_guards.rs`, `manifest_dep_eradication.rs`, `runtime_compat_surface_guard.rs`, `vendored_async_contracts.rs`, and token-scan guard logic in `frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs`.
- Owner split: `ft-xbnl0.1.3` normalizes inherited meaning and closure state; `ft-xbnl0.2.6` turns these into durable actionable guards/doc sweeps instead of stale reminders.

Inherited blocker map
- `ft-riz42` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.1`
- `wa-1m7nk` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.2`
- `wa-16hou` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.3` (note: ProudSalmon already landed a bounded `ipc.rs` support slice on 2026-04-01; do not duplicate it)
- `wa-1u55z` + `wa-3mfv9` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.4`
- `wa-2h1ha` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.6`

Bottom line
- Real shipping-path async debt is fully partitioned across `ft-xbnl0.2.1` / `.2.2` / `.2.3` / `.2.4` / `.2.5` / `.2.6`.
- `ft-xbnl0.1.3` owns inherited-ledger normalization; `ft-xbnl0.1.4` owns the shared proof bar each cutover lane must satisfy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.307225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-04T00:57:40.446072Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T00:56:15.187408Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cutover","goal-line","inventory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.307225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2825,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Validated the async-debt inventory against the current workspace on 2026-04-03.

Corroboration used:
- `mcp__morph_mcp__codebase_search` over remaining direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and `runtime_compat` usage.
- `rg -n '(tokio|smol|async-io|runtime_compat)' Cargo.toml crates frankenterm docs README.md AGENTS.md`.

Confirmed owner partition remains accurate:
- direct vendored/client `smol` + `async-io` shipping-path debt -> `ft-xbnl0.2.1`
- core `runtime_compat` quarantine collapse -> `ft-xbnl0.2.2` / `.2.3` / `.2.4` / `.2.5`
- manifest and permanent guard cleanup -> `ft-xbnl0.2.5` / `.2.6`

Concrete corroborating surfaces still present in code today include:
- `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs` (`smol::Async`, `smol::channel`, `smol::future::or`, `async_io::IoSafe`)
- `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` and multiple `crates/frankenterm-core/src/*` modules importing `runtime_compat::*`
- workspace manifests still carrying direct runtime debt in the locations named in the bead notes

This leaves `ft-xbnl0.1.1` as a completed inventory/ownership ledger, not an implementation lane. Downstream finish-line async work should treat this bead's notes as the authoritative offender map and update them only if the offender set changes materially.
","created_at":"2026-04-04T00:56:15Z"}]}
